name: Waimalu
desc: Large residential area between Aiea and Pearl City — affordable, established homes, quiet streets, great freeway access.
insider: Waimalu Shopping Center is old-school local — the plate lunch spots there serve massive portions for under $10
The neighborhood is one of the most affordable in the Pearl City/Aiea corridor with solid mid-century homes on quiet streets
It's sandwiched between Pearlridge Mall and Pearl City Shopping Center — you're never more than 5 minutes from anything you need
The streets are flat and quiet with wide sidewalks — popular with evening joggers, dog walkers, and families with strollers
Waimalu Stream runs through the neighborhood and floods fast in heavy rains — avoid homes in the low-lying areas near the stream bed
The community is heavily local Japanese and Filipino families who've been here for generations — block parties and neighborhood potlucks are common
Koa Pancake House in Waimalu serves some of the biggest and best breakfast plates in the area — the macadamia nut pancakes are legendary
H-1 on-ramp at Waimalu is one of the less congested Pearl City entries — locals use it to skip the Pearl City/Aiea backup
Neal S. Blaisdell Park along the water has a walking path, picnic areas, and stunning Pearl Harbor sunset views that most people don't know about
The neighborhood association is active and hosts an annual cleanup day — good sign of community pride and involvement
